tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi, I'm new to using one of these memory assistants. Can you show me what memories or information is currently stored in the system? I want to see what's already there before I start adding anything.

Now that I've seen what's stored, I'd like to add some important details about a colleague I work with. Her name is Amara and she's been instrumental in coordinating our water sanitation efforts in the camp. She has a background in engineering and speaks three languages, which has been incredibly valuable when working with different community groups. Could you help me remember this information so I don't lose track of it later?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Colleague named Amara who coordinates water sanitation efforts in a camp. She has an engineering background and speaks three languages, which has been valuable for working with different community groups."}

messages.content:
Done! I've stored the information about Amara in your memories. Here's what was saved:

> **Amara** - A colleague who coordinates water sanitation efforts in the camp. She has an engineering background and speaks three languages, which has been valuable for working with different community groups.

The memory was saved with ID `mem_001`. You'll be able to search for this information later using queries like "Amara," "water sanitation," or "colleague who speaks three languages."

Is there anything else you'd like to add or check on?
Now that I've added Amara's information, can you help me find it again? I want to make sure I can pull up details about her when I need them later.

{"status": "success", "results": [{"id": "mem_001", "text": "Colleague named Amara who coordinates water sanitation efforts in a camp. She has an engineering background and speaks three languages, which has been valuable for working with different community groups.", "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "query": "Amara colleague water sanitation engineering languages", "limit": 3, "found": 1}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
I found your memory about Amara. Here's what's stored:

> **Memory ID:** mem_001  
> **Created:** January 15, 2024 at 10:30 AM
>
> *"Colleague named Amara who coordinates water sanitation efforts in a camp. She has an engineering background and speaks three languages, which has been valuable for working with different community groups."*

The system was able to retrieve it using keywords from your query. Now you know how to find this information whenever you need it—simply search for things like "Amara," "water sanitation," or "engineering background" and it'll come up.

Would you like to add any more details or test searching for something else?

Now that I've added Amara's info and know how to find it again, I should probably clean out some old memories that aren't relevant anymore. Can you show me what's currently stored so I can figure out what to remove?

{"status": "success", "memories": [{"id": "mem_001", "text": "Amara is a colleague who works in water sanitation engineering and speaks multiple languages including English, French, and Arabic", "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "count": 1}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored in your memories:

| ID | Created | Content |
|----|---------|---------|
| **mem_001** | Jan 15, 2024 | Amara is a colleague who works in water sanitation engineering and speaks multiple languages including English, French, and Arabic |

You only have one memory in the system—the one we just added about Amara. There's not much to clean out unless you'd like to remove that memory entirely.

Would you like to:
1. **Delete this memory** about Amara?
2. **Keep it** as is since it's still relevant?

Let me know what you'd like to do!

No need to delete it—Amara's information is still relevant since she's actively coordinating our water sanitation work. Thanks for helping me check on what's stored. That covers what I needed to do.

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! Here's a quick recap of what we did:

- ✅ **Listed** all stored memories (found 1)
- ✅ **Added** information about Amara (your colleague in water sanitation with an engineering background who speaks three languages)
- ✅ **Retrieved** the memory to confirm it can be found later
- ✅ **Reviewed** what's currently stored—keeping it all

You're all set! The memory about Amara is safely stored and ready for you to search whenever you need it. Feel free to reach out if you need help with anything else. 😊
